title: config: use EventsLogger=file without systemd
body:
if systemd is not available, use the file events logger backend.

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Scrivano <gscrivan@redhat.com>

comments.body: LGTM, and (as long as I'm careful to `rm -rf /{,var/}tmp/run-1000`) the test binary is working fine. Thanks @giuseppe for looking at this so quickly.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *giuseppe* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Failures looked flaky, so I restarted them.
LGTM, prune remote tests seem to be flaking all over....
